prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "仰卧起坐" (sit-up) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person at the top of a sit-up: starting from supine position, now sitting up so that the torso is lifted off the ground at about 60-70 degrees from horizontal; both knees bent at 90 degrees with feet flat on the ground; hands placed lightly behind the head with elbows out to the sides (not pulling on the neck); chin tucked slightly, neck neutral; full contraction of the abdominal wall; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: rectus abdominis (front of abdomen, full contraction). Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: hip flexors (psoas, rectus femoris) and obliques. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腹直肌" pointing to the rectus abdominis; "髂腰肌" pointing to the hip flexors; "腹斜肌" pointing to the obliques. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
